subject: Chemistry
title: Quantum Dots: Nano-Sized Color
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Quantum Dots: Nano-Sized Color.

hook.text: Quantum dots are crystals so small that their color depends on their size. A 2 nm dot glows blue; a 6 nm dot glows red.

buildup.text: At the nanoscale, quantum confinement restricts electron energy levels. Smaller dots have larger energy gaps.

discovery.text: Larger gap means higher-energy light (blue). Smaller gap means lower-energy light (red). Size tunes color precisely.

twist.text: Samsung and Sony use quantum dots in QLED TVs. They produce purer colors than any other display technology.

climax.text: The 2023 Chemistry Nobel went to quantum dot discoverers: Moungi Bawendi, Louis Brus, and Alexei Ekimov.

punchline.text: Shrink a crystal, change its color. Size controls what wavelength it emits.

quiz.question: How do quantum dots produce different colors?
options: Smaller dots have larger energy gaps and emit bluer light
Different chemical elements produce different colors
The dots are dyed with traditional pigments at nanoscale
